title: Aifanar Energy Pvt.Ltd vs Jethalal Lakhamshi Patel on 2 July, 2021
body: Aifanar Energy Pvt.Ltd vs Jethalal Lakhamshi Patel on 2 July, 2021 Author: N.V.Anjaria Bench: N.V.Anjaria C/CRA/162/2021 ORDER DATED: 02/07/2021 IN THE HIGH COURT OF GUJARAT AT AHMEDABAD R/CIVIL REVISION APPLICATION NO. 162 of 2021 ========================================================== AIFANAR ENERGY PVT.LTD Versus JETHALAL LAKHAMSHI PATEL ========================================================== Appearance: MS MEGHA JANI(1028) for the Applicant(s) No. 1 for the Opponent(s) No. 1,2,3,4 ========================================================== CORAM:HONOURABLE MR. JUSTICE N.V.ANJARIA Date : 02/07/2021 ORAL ORDER Heard learned advocate Ms. Megha Jani for the applicant. 2. This Revision Application is filed against order dated 30.3.2021 below Exhibit 12 in Regular Civil Suit No. 35 of 2021 passed by learned Principal Senior Civil Judge, Nakhatrana, Kutch, whereby the application of the petitioner, who was original defendant, under Order VII Rule 11 of the Code of Civil Procedure , 1908 for rejection of the plaint came to be dismissed. 3. Referring to the nature and the prayers made in the Regular Civil Suit of the plaintiff, learned advocate for the petitioner herein relies on section 20A read with section 41(ha) of the Specific Relief Act to submit that the defendant company is engaged in the business of wind mill for generation of electricity. It was therefore submitted that it falls under the category of infrastructural project mentioned in the Schedule to the Specific Relief Act , for which granting of injunction whether perpetual or temporary, would be barred. Page 1 of 2 Downloaded on : Sat Jul 03 05:19:29 IST 2021 C/CRA/162/2021 ORDER DATED: 02/07/2021 3.1 It was submitted that the same ground was raised in application under Order VII Rule 11, CPC , which came to be not accepted by the court below on the reasoning that existence of the contract between the parties relating to the dispute, was a condition precedent for application of provision, however, there was no such express or implied contract. Learned advocate for the petitioner relies on the decision of the Delhi High Court in Hari Ram Nagar and others vs. Delhi Development Authority and others [(2019) SCC online Delhi 9747]. 4. Notice, returnable on 16.7.2021. 5. It is observed that if the defendant prays for adjournment in the aforesaid suit, such request shall be granted and proceedings of the suit shall be adjourned by the court concerned. This direction shall operate till the next date. Direct service is permitted. (N.V.ANJARIA, J) C.M. JOSHI Page 2 of 2 Downloaded on : Sat Jul 03 05:19:29 IST 2021
